questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a box-and-whisker plot in sports statistics?

options.B: To show the distribution of scores

questions.question: The $25^{th}$ percentile in a set of marathon finish times is $3:15$. What does this represent?

options.D: The time below which $25%$ of runners finished
options.E: The time above which $25%$ of runners finished

questions.question: In a box-and-whisker plot of basketball scores, an outlier is a score that is $1.5 cdot IQR$ above the third quartile. What does IQR represent?

options.A: The difference between the highest and lowest scores
options.B: The range of the middle $50%$ of scores

questions.question: A set of data on cycling speeds has a first quartile of $15$ mph and a third quartile of $25$ mph. What is the interquartile range?
Which of the following box-and-whisker plots indicates the presence of an outlier?

options.A: A plot with a long whisker on the left side
options.B: A plot with a long whisker on the right side
options.C: A plot with a short box and long whiskers
options.D: A plot with a long box and short whiskers

questions.question: A golfer's scores for $9$ holes are $3, 4, 5, 4, 3, 5, 6, 4, 5$. What is the second quartile (median) of these scores?
The $50^{th}$ percentile in a set of swim meet times is $2:30$. What does this represent?

options.D: The time below which $50%$ of swimmers finished
options.E: The time above which $50%$ of swimmers finished

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about the interquartile range (IQR) in a set of data?

options.A: It is the difference between the highest and lowest values
options.B: It is the average of the first and third quartiles
options.C: It is the range of the middle $50%$ of values
options.D: It is the median of the data set
options.E: It is the mode of the data set

questions.question: Construct a box-and-whisker plot for the following set of data on jogging times (in minutes): $25, 30, 20, 35, 40, 28, 32, 38, 42, 45$. Identify the median, first quartile, third quartile, and any outliers.

A set of data on tennis serve speeds has the following five-number summary: min $50$ mph, $Q_1 60$ mph, median $65$ mph, $Q_3 70$ mph, max $80$ mph. Sketch a box-and-whisker plot for this data and explain what it reveals about the distribution of serve speeds.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of quartiles and interquartile range
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of identifying outliers in a data set
Tip 3: Encourage students to use real-world examples from sports to illustrate their understanding of box-and-whisker plots
